---
title: "Genre Decoded"
format:
  html:
    theme: none
    css: styles.css
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
---

<!--  
The scrollytelling document
# 1. Kill any existing preview processes (if any)
killall quarto 2>/dev/null || true

# 2. Clean the cache and the output folder
rm -rf .quarto/ _site/

# 3. Render and force the output name
quarto render index.qmd --to html --output _site/index.html
-->

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     HERO
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
<div class="hero">
  <div class="hero-label">A Spotify Data Story</div>
  <h1>Genres have <em>personalities</em> — but not as different as you'd think.</h1>
  <p class="hero-sub">
    We analysed 28,000 songs across 6 genres using Spotify's audio fingerprints.
    Here's what we found.
  </p>
  <div class="scroll-hint">Scroll to explore</div>
</div>

<div class="section-divider"></div>

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     SECTION: WHAT MAKES A GENRE?
     Layout: prose steps on left, sticky chart panel on right
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->
::: {#genre-scrolly-container}

<section class="scroll-section" id="genre-personality">
  <div class="scrolly" id="genre-scrolly">
    <div class="scrolly-steps" id="genre-steps">
      <div class="step is-active" data-step="intro">
         <h2>What makes a genre?</h2>
         <p>...</p>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="scrolly-sticky">
      <div class="chart-panel" id="main-chart-panel">
        <div id="genre-legend"></div>
        <div id="radar-container"></div>
        <div id="feature-filters"></div>
        <div id="bar-container"></div>
      </div>
    </div>

  </div>
</section>

:::

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     OJS: All reactive chart logic
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->

```{ojs}
//| output: false

// ── 0. Load data
genreProfiles = FileAttachment("data/processed/genre_profiles.json").json()
topSongs      = FileAttachment("data/processed/top_songs.json").json()

// ── 1. Constants
FEATURES = ["Danceability", "Energy", "Valence", "Acousticness", "Tempo", "Speechiness"]

// ── 2. Reactive State
// ── 2. Shared reactive state ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
viewof selectedGenres = {
  const container = document.getElementById("genre-legend")
  const input = html`<input type="hidden">`;
  // Initial state: all genres selected
  // Create a default state so the rest of the code has data to work with
  const state = new Set(genreProfiles ? genreProfiles.map(d => d.genre) : []);
  input.value = state;

  if (!container) {
    console.warn("OJS: #genre-legend not found in HTML yet.");
    return input; // Return the input anyway so OJS keeps moving
  }

  genreProfiles.forEach(d => {
    const item = container.appendChild(document.createElement("div"))
    item.className = "legend-item active"
    item.style.borderColor = d.color
    
    const dot = item.appendChild(document.createElement("div"))
    dot.className = "legend-dot"
    dot.style.background = d.color

    const label = item.appendChild(document.createElement("span"))
    label.textContent = d.genre.toUpperCase()

    item.addEventListener("click", () => {
      if (state.has(d.genre)) {
        state.delete(d.genre);
        item.classList.remove("active");
        item.style.borderColor = "transparent";
        dot.style.opacity = "0.3";
      } else {
        state.add(d.genre);
        item.classList.add("active");
        item.style.borderColor = d.color;
        dot.style.opacity = "1";
      }
      // Re-wrap in a new Set so OJS detects the change
      input.value = new Set(state);
      input.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", {bubbles: true}));
    });
  })
  return input
}

viewof selectedFeature = {
  const container = document.getElementById("feature-filters");
  const input = html`<input type="hidden">`;
  input.value = null;

  if (!container) return input;
  container.innerHTML = "";
  let activeBtn = null;

  FEATURES.forEach(feat => {
    const btn = container.appendChild(document.createElement("button"));
    btn.className = "filter-btn";
    btn.textContent = feat;

    btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
      if (activeBtn === btn) {
        btn.classList.remove("active");
        activeBtn = null;
        input.value = null;
      } else {
        if (activeBtn) activeBtn.classList.remove("active");
        btn.classList.add("active");
        activeBtn = btn;
        input.value = feat;
      }
      input.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true }));
    });
  });

  return input;
}

// ── 3. Filtered data (FIXED SYNTAX) ──────────────────────────────────────
filteredProfiles = {
  if (selectedGenres && typeof selectedGenres.has === "function") {
    return genreProfiles.filter(d => selectedGenres.has(d.genre));
  }
  return genreProfiles;
}

// ── 4. Radar chart (RESPONSIVE) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
{
  const container = document.getElementById("radar-container")
  if (!container) return

  const W = width > 900 ? (width / 4.5) : (width - 40);
  const H = W;
  const cx = W / 2, cy = H / 2
  const R  = W * 0.35
  const N  = FEATURES.length
  const angles = FEATURES.map((_, i) => (Math.PI * 2 * i) / N - Math.PI / 2)

  const pt = (feat, val) => {
    const i = FEATURES.indexOf(feat)
    const a = angles[i]
    const r = val * R
    return [cx + r * Math.cos(a), cy + r * Math.sin(a)]
  }

  const svgNS = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  const svg = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "svg")
  svg.setAttribute("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
  svg.setAttribute("width", W)
  svg.setAttribute("height", H)
  svg.style.overflow = "visible"

  // Grid rings
  [0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1.0].forEach(r => {
    const ring = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "polygon")
    ring.setAttribute("points", angles.map(a => `${cx + r*R*Math.cos(a)},${cy + r*R*Math.sin(a)}`).join(" "))
    ring.setAttribute("fill", "none")
    ring.setAttribute("stroke", "#2a2a2a")
    svg.appendChild(ring)
  })

  // Polygons
  genreProfiles.forEach(d => {
    const isSelected = selectedGenres.has(d.genre)
    const pts = FEATURES.map(f => pt(f, d.features[f] ?? 0)).join(" ")
    const poly = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "polygon")
    poly.setAttribute("points", pts)
    poly.setAttribute("fill", d.color)
    poly.setAttribute("fill-opacity", isSelected ? "0.15" : "0.02")
    poly.setAttribute("stroke", d.color)
    poly.setAttribute("stroke-width", isSelected ? "2.5" : "0.5")
    poly.style.transition = "all 0.4s ease"
    svg.appendChild(poly)
  })

  container.innerHTML = ""
  container.appendChild(svg)
}

// ── 5. Grouped bar chart (RESPONSIVE) ──────────────────────────────────────────
{
  const container = document.getElementById("bar-container")
  if (!container) return

  const W = width > 900 ? (width / 4.5) : (width - 40);
  const H = 200
  const margin = { top: 16, right: 8, bottom: 40, left: 28 }
  const innerW = W - margin.left - margin.right
  const innerH = H - margin.top - margin.bottom

  const svgNS = "http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
  const svg = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "svg")
  svg.setAttribute("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
  svg.setAttribute("width", W)

  const g = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "g")
  g.setAttribute("transform", `translate(${margin.left},${margin.top})`)
  svg.appendChild(g)

  const featW = innerW / FEATURES.length
  const genreW = featW / genreProfiles.length

  FEATURES.forEach((feat, fi) => {
    const isHighlighted = selectedFeature === feat || selectedFeature === null
    
    genreProfiles.forEach((d, gi) => {
      const isGenreOn = selectedGenres.has(d.genre)
      const val = d.features[feat] ?? 0
      const rect = document.createElementNS(svgNS, "rect")
      rect.setAttribute("x", fi * featW + gi * genreW)
      rect.setAttribute("y", innerH - (val * innerH))
      rect.setAttribute("width", Math.max(genreW - 2, 1))
      rect.setAttribute("height", val * innerH)
      rect.setAttribute("fill", d.color)
      rect.setAttribute("opacity", isHighlighted && isGenreOn ? 0.8 : 0.05)
      g.appendChild(rect)
    })
  })

  container.innerHTML = ""
  container.appendChild(svg)
}

```

<!-- ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
     Scrollama: activates .step prose cards as user scrolls
     ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ -->

```{=html}
<script type="module">
import scrollama from "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/scrollama@3.2.0/+esm"

function initScroller() {
  const scroller = scrollama();
  const steps = document.querySelectorAll("#genre-steps .step");

  // Only initialize if the steps actually exist in the DOM
  if (steps.length > 0) {
    scroller
      .setup({
        step: "#genre-steps .step",
        offset: 0.5,
        debug: false
      })
      .onStepEnter((response) => {
        window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("step-change", { 
          detail: response.element.dataset.step 
        }));
        steps.forEach(s => s.classList.remove('is-active'));
        response.element.classList.add('is-active');
      });

    window.addEventListener("resize", scroller.resize);
    console.log("Scrollama initialized successfully.");
  } else {
    // If not ready, try again in 100ms
    setTimeout(initScroller, 100);
  }
}

// Start the check
initScroller();
</script>
```
